messages.author: emptiness23
messages.text: We've got some yagis coming in (hopefully) this week to establish a link between a few repeaters. If those improve SNR enough that the mesh is reliable, we hope to move to SF=10 and have it still be reliable. That will ensure that people who are new to MeshCore and are going with the SF=10 default for their nodes can play with us
messages.content_html: We've got some yagis coming in (hopefully) this week to establish a link between a few repeaters. If those improve SNR enough that the mesh is reliable, we hope to move to SF=10 and have it still be reliable. That will ensure that people who are new to MeshCore and are going with the SF=10 default for their nodes can play with us

messages.author: emptiness23
messages.text: Well, a couple of us installed those yagis a few weeks ago, and we can occasionally hear each other at SF=11, so… progress?
messages.content_html: Well, a couple of us installed those yagis a few weeks ago, and we can occasionally hear each other at SF=11, so… progress?
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: emptiness23
messages.text: There are now 3 distinct MeshCore islands that we know about, and we're looking at places to put a repeater so we can get two of those islands connected. The remaining one is significantly distant from the others, so that might be a while before we have those linked
messages.content_html: There are now 3 distinct MeshCore islands that we know about, and we're looking at places to put a repeater so we can get two of those islands connected. The remaining one is significantly distant from the others, so that might be a while before we have those linked

messages.author: Macross
messages.text: like frikkin never at the rate of adoption. but we can dream.
messages.content_html: like frikkin never at the rate of adoption. but we can dream.

messages.author: emptiness23
messages.text: Reminder to anyone who happens to be in metro Detroit on this server that we need more repeater nodes! We are spread across eastern and southeastern Oakland County, but if you put one up with an antenna high enough, you might hear one of us. 910.525 MHz, SF=11
messages.content_html: Reminder to anyone who happens to be in metro Detroit on this server that we need more repeater nodes! We are spread across eastern and southeastern Oakland County, but if you put one up with an antenna high enough, you might hear one of us. 910.525 MHz, SF=11

messages.author: KD619 | WRTP691
messages.text: I am currently building some solar repeaters. What height should I aim for mounting them? I mean obviously as high as possible. But what should the minimum be so that it’s worth installing them? I’m in the Westland area.
messages.content_html: I am currently building some solar repeaters. What height should I aim for mounting them? I mean obviously as high as possible. But what should the minimum be so that it’s worth installing them? I’m in the Westland area.

messages.author: MrWeavis
messages.text: Hello, I'm in the Toledo area where it's very lonely. I have a repeater running a G2, 8 DBi omni at 30 feet. Fingers crossed that I see a band opening once in a while like I used to get on MT. (edited)
messages.content_html: Hello, I'm in the Toledo area where it's very lonely. I have a repeater running a G2, 8 DBi omni at 30 feet. Fingers crossed that I see a band opening once in a while like I used to get on MT. (edited)

messages.author: KD619 | WRTP691
messages.text: Mine is currently mounted about 35ft up. I have a 5dbi antenna on it at the moment. Not currently able to communicate with anyone other than my own nodes in a roughly 2-3 mile radius. Debating if I should try a higher gain antenna to potentially hit a repeater about 15 miles away. However I don’t even know if that repeater still exists as it was added to the map several months ago

messages.author: KD619 | WRTP691
messages.text: On my meshtastic node that’s mounted about 30ft. I was able to communicate with a node near Port Clinton. I think it’s roughly 70 miles. However I was only able to maintain communication for about 30 minutes one night.
messages.content_html: On my meshtastic node that’s mounted about 30ft. I was able to communicate with a node near Port Clinton. I think it’s roughly 70 miles. However I was only able to maintain communication for about 30 minutes one night.

messages.text: I always enjoyed the band openings on MT but have yet to see any on Meshcore. Grand Rapids Mi to Toledo was one of my best.

messages.author: KD619 | WRTP691
messages.text: Any updates on this? Are these repeaters still up? Have you guys expanded at all? I have a repeated up in Westland and would like to work on expanding the mesh in the area. I'm open to advice on how to best expand and plan repeater placement.
messages.content_html: Any updates on this? Are these repeaters still up? Have you guys expanded at all? I have a repeated up in Westland and would like to work on expanding the mesh in the area. I'm open to advice on how to best expand and plan repeater placement.
messages.reply_html: Original message was deleted or could not be loaded.

messages.author: Macross
messages.text: nope. honestly we all got bored with it and moved on to other things. all our sticks are still in the air, but no other locals have popped up around us to help fill in the gaps.
messages.content_html: nope. honestly we all got bored with it and moved on to other things. all our sticks are still in the air, but no other locals have popped up around us to help fill in the gaps.
messages.reply_html:
KD619 | WRTP691
Any updates on this? Are these repeaters still up? Have you guys expanded at all? I have a repeated up in Westland and would like to work on expanding the mesh in the area. I'm open to advice on how to best expand and plan repeater placement.

messages.author: Macross
messages.text: your money, but I wouldnt invest too heavily in it. Also, no one with a tower is gonna let you just throw some rando node up there. anything that goes up on the big radio towers or on top of buildings and stuff has to be built to withstand harsh conditions, and tested and documented to not interfere with the stuff thats up there, specially the stuff paying rent.
messages.content_html: your money, but I wouldnt invest too heavily in it. Also, no one with a tower is gonna let you just throw some rando node up there. anything that goes up on the big radio towers or on top of buildings and stuff has to be built to withstand harsh conditions, and tested and documented to not interfere with the stuff thats up there, specially the stuff paying rent.

messages.text: hi y'all, barging in here from Charlotte, NC area. I am building some radios for family up north, and was wondering if anyone knows if Mt. Brighton has any towers on it and if anyone has possibly looked into getting a repeater installed there.

messages.author: Nikleberry
messages.text: Just flashed my roof node yesterday in northeast Warren on 910.525. only 15" of elevation. Set up as a repeater, but not seeing anything on the map. Seems MT has a much higer adoption rate in SE MI

messages.author: Huffdaddy82
messages.text: New here, I have been setting up a Meshtastic network but am doing an identical MeshCore setup. Repeater gear should start to arrive tomorrow (yeah amazon is delivering on Easter!) I won't have my antenna and cable for a couple of weeks because the ones on Amazon for repeaters are junk for the most part. I will set up a repeater at 30' and hope I can help this grow in our area.
messages.content_html: New here, I have been setting up a Meshtastic network but am doing an identical MeshCore setup. Repeater gear should start to arrive tomorrow (yeah amazon is delivering on Easter!) I won't have my antenna and cable for a couple of weeks because the ones on Amazon for repeaters are junk for the most part. I will set up a repeater at 30' and hope I can help this grow in our area.

messages.author: Nerobergstr
messages.text: New here. Started with Meshtastic, but have moved all my HW to Meshcore. I have a few repeaters running standard US settings and am covering a fair amount of downtown Lake Orion. Have not been able to connect with others in the area yet. My brother has a 30 km link established in Central Michigan covering much of the Coleman area. Still pretty lonely, but I think we’ll get there eventually.
messages.content_html: New here. Started with Meshtastic, but have moved all my HW to Meshcore. I have a few repeaters running standard US settings and am covering a fair amount of downtown Lake Orion. Have not been able to connect with others in the area yet. My brother has a 30 km link established in Central Michigan covering much of the Coleman area. Still pretty lonely, but I think we’ll get there eventually.

messages.author: Uncle Lit
messages.text: the meshcore dev team have moved to a new discord server. details and reasons are here: https://blog.meshcore.io/2026/04/23/the-split Please create a new thread for your region in the https://discord.com/channels/1495203904898728149/1495412453150818585 for your region on the new meshcore.io discord server. https://meshcore.gg
